title: Asterocephalus atropurpureus

taxonomy: Asterocephalus atropurpureus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of China and is known for its unique purple flowers. The plant's classification is further categorized under the genus Asterocephalus, which is a monotypic genus containing only one species. Within the Gentianaceae family, Asterocephalus atropurpureus is part of the tribe Gentianeae. Its classification is an essential aspect of understanding the plant's evolution and relationship with other species in the Gentianaceae family.

The plant's roots and leaves are reportedly used to treat various health conditions, including fever and respiratory issues.
